title: Remembering Dame Jenni Murray: A Radio Star Who Talked to Women
summary: Dame Jenni Murray was a famous radio presenter who hosted a show called Woman's Hour on BBC Radio 4 for over 30 years. She started in 1987 and left in 2020, making her the longest-serving host. She talked to many important women like Margaret Thatcher and Hillary Clinton, helping people discuss women's lives and issues. She was known for being warm, brave, and smart. Sadly, she died at age 75. People at the BBC said she was a broadcasting icon and will be missed. She grew up in Barnsley and studied French and drama before starting her radio career in 1973. Her work inspired many listeners.

background_read: Dame Jenni Murray was a well-known British broadcaster who dedicated her career to discussing women's issues on the radio. She hosted Woman's Hour, a popular BBC program that focuses on topics important to women, from 1987 to 2020. Her interviews with famous figures helped listeners learn about different perspectives. She started in local radio and worked her way up, becoming an icon in broadcasting. Her death is a loss to many who enjoyed her shows.
Article_Structure: WHO: Dame Jenni Murray, a radio presenter
WHAT: She hosted Woman's Hour for over 30 years and died at 75
WHERE: On BBC Radio 4 and in Barnsley, UK
WHY: To share women's stories and inspire conversations

perspectives.description: She was celebrated as a legendary figure in radio for her long service and impact.
